cases.prompt_body: Tight documentary close-up of the Challenger solid rocket booster field joint and O-ring seat, canonical cylindrical steel geometry remains intact and legible, the framing stays aligned with the accepted field-joint band closeup, one thin pale gray smoke seep emerges from the ring interface and curls only slightly while the rest of the hardware stays calm and pre-failure, no flame, no soot streak, no cutaway diagram, no exploded view, no tools, no hands, parchment beige, muted blue, restrained industrial stillness

Exterior Space Shuttle Challenger in flight high above Earth about one minute after launch, orbiter, rust-orange external tank, and twin white boosters remain fully canonical and immediately recognizable, curved Earth and cloud field lie far below, one bright flame becomes visible again along the right booster joint path while all sustained exhaust remains aft-only and physically correct, no breakup, no detached debris, no explosion spectacle, cool upper-atmosphere blue, restrained documentary tension

Historically recognizable exterior Challenger breakup in open sky above Earth, the orbiter, rust-orange external tank, twin white boosters, and branching breach plume read as the real event rather than an abstract split, plume geometry stays specific and documentary while remaining restrained, no mission-control framing, no poster blast, no generic rocket silhouette, cool daylight, ash gray, smoke white, charcoal, signal ember, severe documentary stillness
